Apple___Apple_scab.symptoms: Velvety brown to olive-green spots on leaves, turning black over time. Leaves may yellow and drop early.
Apple___Apple_scab.organic: Spray a solution of liquid copper or sulfur before blossoms open. Rake and destroy fallen leaves in autumn to prevent overwintering spores.
Apple___Apple_scab.chemical: Apply Captan or Myclobutanil fungicides during wet spring weather.
Apple___Apple_scab.prevention: Prune trees to improve air circulation, choose scab-resistant varieties, and clean up all fallen orchard debris.

Apple___Black_rot.symptoms: Purple spots on leaves that expand to brown circles with purple borders (frog-eye leaf spot). Fruit develops black decaying spots.
Apple___Black_rot.organic: Prune out dead wood, cankers, and mummified fruit on the tree. Spray copper fungicides early in the season.
Apple___Black_rot.chemical: Use Captan or Thiophanate-methyl fungicides during the pre-bloom and bloom periods.
Apple___Black_rot.prevention: Remove dead wood immediately from the orchard, avoid wounding bark, and maintain high tree vigor.

Apple___Cedar_apple_rust.symptoms: Bright orange-yellow spots on upper leaf surfaces. Orange tubular projections develop on lower surfaces.
Apple___Cedar_apple_rust.organic: Remove nearby wild red cedar or juniper trees within 1 mile. Spray copper or sulfur fungicide when buds begin to break.
Apple___Cedar_apple_rust.chemical: Apply Myclobutanil or Chlorothalonil when apple buds show pink color in early spring.
Apple___Cedar_apple_rust.prevention: Select rust-resistant apple cultivars and prune to keep tree canopy open and dry.

Cherry_(including_sour)___Powdery_mildew.symptoms: White, powdery fungal patches on young leaves and twigs. Leaves may curl upward and drop prematurely.
Cherry_(including_sour)___Powdery_mildew.organic: Apply potassium bicarbonate or Neem oil spray at first sign of infection. Ensure plants are in full sun.
Cherry_(including_sour)___Powdery_mildew.chemical: Use Myclobutanil or sulfur-based fungicides during late spring/early summer.
Cherry_(including_sour)___Powdery_mildew.prevention: Prune trees to maximize air flow and sunlight penetration, and avoid high nitrogen fertilizers which cause tender susceptible growth.

Corn_(maize)___Cercospora_leaf_spot Gray.symptoms: Rectangular, tan-to-gray spots on leaves running parallel to leaf veins. Can completely dry out leaves.
Corn_(maize)___Cercospora_leaf_spot Gray.organic: Apply fermented compost tea or biological control agents like Trichoderma. Incorporate residue deep into the soil after harvest.
Corn_(maize)___Cercospora_leaf_spot Gray.chemical: Apply Azoxystrobin or Pyraclostrobin fungicides if disease pressure is high during silking.
Corn_(maize)___Cercospora_leaf_spot Gray.prevention: Practice a 2-year crop rotation with non-host crops like legumes, and plant resistant hybrid varieties.

Corn_(maize)___Common_rust_.symptoms: Golden-brown to reddish-orange powdery pustules on both upper and lower surfaces of leaf blades.
Corn_(maize)___Common_rust_.organic: Dust leaves with sulfur powder or spray Neem oil extract. Ensure good weed control to remove alternate hosts.
Corn_(maize)___Common_rust_.chemical: Fungicides are rarely economical but Pyraclostrobin or Propiconazole can be used on high-value seed crops.
Corn_(maize)___Common_rust_.prevention: Use resistant maize hybrids and sow early in the season to avoid late-summer peak spore loads.

Corn_(maize)___Northern_Leaf_Blight.symptoms: Long, cigar-shaped grayish-green or tan lesions on leaves, starting from lower leaves and moving upward.
Corn_(maize)___Northern_Leaf_Blight.organic: Spray a solution of Bacillus subtilis or copper hydroxide. Shred and bury crop residues after harvest.
Corn_(maize)___Northern_Leaf_Blight.chemical: Apply Strobilurin or Triazole group fungicides at the first sign of lesions before tasseling.
Corn_(maize)___Northern_Leaf_Blight.prevention: Crop rotation with chickpea or peanut, and deep plowing to decompose previous crop residues.

Grape___Black_rot.symptoms: Small brown spots on leaves with dark borders. Grapes turn into shriveled, hard, black mummies.
Grape___Black_rot.organic: Remove all dried mummified grapes from vines. Spray copper-based fungicides when new shoots are 2 to 10 inches long.
Grape___Black_rot.chemical: Apply Mancozeb or Myclobutanil beginning at bud break and repeat every 10-14 days.
Grape___Black_rot.prevention: Prune shoots to encourage maximum airflow, keep vineyard ground free of leaf litter, and ensure vines are elevated.

Grape___Esca_(Black_Measles).symptoms: Interveinal striping on leaves resembling a 'tiger-stripe' pattern of yellow and brown. Fruit develops dark spots.
Grape___Esca_(Black_Measles).organic: Protect pruning wounds with organic sealants or Trichoderma harzianum paste. Remove and burn heavily infected vines.
Grape___Esca_(Black_Measles).chemical: No direct curative chemical spray exists; protect pruning cuts with paint containing thiophanate-methyl.
Grape___Esca_(Black_Measles).prevention: Prune vines during dry weather to minimize wound infections, and avoid overhead sprinkler irrigation.

Grape___Leaf_blight_(Isariopsis_Leaf_Spo.symptoms: Dull brown, irregular spots on leaves. Leaves turn dry, brittle, and drop off early, reducing vine strength.
Grape___Leaf_blight_(Isariopsis_Leaf_Spo.organic: Spray home-made baking soda (potassium bicarbonate) or dilute Neem oil solution. Clean fallen leaves under trellis.
Grape___Leaf_blight_(Isariopsis_Leaf_Spo.chemical: Use Copper Oxychloride or Mancozeb sprays at 15-day intervals during warm humid weather.
Grape___Leaf_blight_(Isariopsis_Leaf_Spo.prevention: Maintain wider vine spacing (at least 8-10 feet) to keep canopy dry, and avoid over-fertilization with nitrogen.

Orange___Haunglongbing_(Citrus_greening).symptoms: Mottled yellowing of leaves, small lopsided bitter fruits with green bottoms. Leaf veins look swollen and yellow.
Orange___Haunglongbing_(Citrus_greening).organic: Apply biological insecticides like Beauveria bassiana to control the Asian citrus psyllid insect carrier. Boost tree immunity with liquid compost.
Orange___Haunglongbing_(Citrus_greening).chemical: No cure exists for the bacteria; control vector psyllids using systemic Imidacloprid or Thiamethoxam.
Orange___Haunglongbing_(Citrus_greening).prevention: Only plant certified disease-free nursery stock, immediately remove and burn infected trees to protect healthy ones.

Peach___Bacterial_spot.symptoms: Water-soaked, purple-to-brown spots on leaves, often falling out to leave a 'shot-hole' appearance. Fruit develops cracks.
Peach___Bacterial_spot.organic: Spray copper-based sprays during dormancy in late autumn and early spring. Avoid excessive nitrogen fertilizer.
Peach___Bacterial_spot.chemical: Apply Oxytetracycline or copper sprays at 7-10 day intervals during wet warm spring conditions.
Peach___Bacterial_spot.prevention: Plant bacterial-spot-resistant peach varieties and prune trees to allow rapid drying of leaves.

Pepper,_bell___Bacterial_spot.symptoms: Small, dark, circular spots with yellow halos on leaves, causing them to yellow and drop rapidly.
Pepper,_bell___Bacterial_spot.organic: Spray organic copper hydroxide. Dip seeds in warm water (50°C) for 25 minutes before planting to disinfect.
Pepper,_bell___Bacterial_spot.chemical: Use a mixture of Copper fungicide and Mancozeb to control resistant strains.
Pepper,_bell___Bacterial_spot.prevention: Practice a 3-year crop rotation, avoid overhead watering, and weed out alternate solanaceous weed hosts.

Potato___Early_blight.symptoms: Dark brown spots with concentric target-board rings, starting on older lower leaves. Lower leaves eventually die.
Potato___Early_blight.organic: Spray copper fungicides or biological sprays like Bacillus subtilis. Apply rich organic compost to boost plant vigor.
Potato___Early_blight.chemical: Apply Chlorothalonil, Mancozeb, or Azoxystrobin at 7-10 day intervals when weather is humid.
Potato___Early_blight.prevention: Practice a 3-year rotation with legumes/grains, irrigate early in the morning so foliage dries quickly, and remove old crop residue.

Potato___Late_blight.symptoms: Large, dark water-soaked spots with white fungal growth on leaf undersides in wet weather. Stems blacken and rot.
Potato___Late_blight.organic: Apply copper sprays immediately at first warning. Destroy all volunteer potato plants. Remove infected plants from the plot.
Potato___Late_blight.chemical: Use systemic fungicides like Metalaxyl-M or protectants like Mancozeb when blight warnings are issued.
Potato___Late_blight.prevention: Only plant certified disease-free seed tubers, avoid overhead irrigation, and harvest only when vines are completely dead.

Squash___Powdery_mildew.symptoms: White, powdery talcum-like spots on leaves and stems. Infected leaves eventually dry out, turn brown, and wither.
Squash___Powdery_mildew.organic: Spray milk-water mixture (40% milk, 60% water) on leaves in full sun, or use Potassium Bicarbonate and Neem oil.
Squash___Powdery_mildew.chemical: Apply Triadimefon or Carbendazim at the first sign of white patches.
Squash___Powdery_mildew.prevention: Ensure wide spacing for gourds, plant in areas with direct sunlight, and select resistant cultivars.

Strawberry___Leaf_scorch.symptoms: Dark purple spots that expand into large reddish-purple blotches. Leaves dry up, curl, and look scorched.
Strawberry___Leaf_scorch.organic: Remove infected leaves. Spray copper or sulfur-based organic fungicides during warm damp periods.
Strawberry___Leaf_scorch.chemical: Use Thiophanate-methyl or Captan fungicides before blossoms open.
Strawberry___Leaf_scorch.prevention: Plant in raised beds to ensure rapid drying, avoid overhead sprinklers, and replace old strawberry beds every 3 years.

Tomato___Bacterial_spot.symptoms: Small, dark greasy spots on leaves with yellow borders. Leaves eventually turn yellow, wither, and drop off.
Tomato___Bacterial_spot.organic: Spray copper hydroxide or biological sprays containing Bacillus amyloliquefaciens. Mulch soil surface to prevent soil splash.
Tomato___Bacterial_spot.chemical: Use Copper Sulfate mixed with Mancozeb to manage copper-resistant strains.
Tomato___Bacterial_spot.prevention: Avoid working in the field when plants are wet, buy certified disease-free seeds, and rotate crops out of the tomato family.

Tomato___Early_blight.symptoms: Leathery black spots with concentric target-board rings on lower leaves. Causes leaf yellowing and dropping.
Tomato___Early_blight.organic: Prune lower leaves up to 12 inches from the ground to prevent soil splash. Spray organic copper or Neem oil regularly.
Tomato___Early_blight.chemical: Apply Mancozeb, Chlorothalonil, or Azoxystrobin at 10-day intervals.
Tomato___Early_blight.prevention: Mulch the soil heavily with straw, keep plant spacing wide for air circulation, and water at the roots using drip irrigation.

Tomato___Late_blight.symptoms: Large, dark greasy spots on leaves that rot quickly in cool wet weather. A fuzzy white mold appears on leaf bottoms.
Tomato___Late_blight.organic: Apply organic copper spray immediately upon detection. Prune severely infected branches and dispose of them far from the farm.
Tomato___Late_blight.chemical: Use systemic Chlorothalonil or Metalaxyl-M during cool, cloudy, and wet periods.
Tomato___Late_blight.prevention: Do not plant potatoes near tomatoes, keep foliage dry by watering early, and maintain wide plant spacing.

Tomato___Leaf_Mold.symptoms: Pale green or yellow spots on upper leaf surfaces. Olive-green to purple velvety mold grows on corresponding leaf undersides.
Tomato___Leaf_Mold.organic: Spray sulfur or baking soda solution. Maximize greenhouse ventilation. Prune lower foliage to keep the ground dry.
Tomato___Leaf_Mold.chemical: Use Chlorothalonil or Difenoconazole fungicides if humidity remains high.
Tomato___Leaf_Mold.prevention: Ensure high air circulation, avoid wet foliage overnight, and use drip irrigation instead of overhead watering.

Tomato___Septoria_leaf_spot.symptoms: Numerous small circular spots with grey centers and dark brown margins on lower leaves. Tiny black specks in the spots.
Tomato___Septoria_leaf_spot.organic: Prune lower leaves to remove early infections. Apply copper fungicide. Mulch the soil heavily to contain soil-borne spores.
Tomato___Septoria_leaf_spot.chemical: Use Chlorothalonil, Mancozeb, or copper-based sprays every 7-14 days.
Tomato___Septoria_leaf_spot.prevention: Rotate crops (avoid tomatoes, potatoes, and eggplants for 2 years in that plot) and keep weeds under control.

Tomato___Spider_mites Two-spotted_spider.symptoms: Fine yellow speckling or stippling on leaf surfaces. Fine silken webbing on leaf undersides and stems. Leaves turn bronze and dry.
Tomato___Spider_mites Two-spotted_spider.organic: Spray with insecticidal soap, Neem oil, or release predatory mites (Phytoseiulus persimilis). Spray leaves with sharp water streams.
Tomato___Spider_mites Two-spotted_spider.chemical: Apply Abamectin or Spiromesifen miticides if infestation is severe. Avoid standard pyrethroids which kill beneficial insects.
Tomato___Spider_mites Two-spotted_spider.prevention: Keep plants well-watered as drought-stressed plants are highly susceptible to mites, and control dusty weeds around the plot.

Tomato___Target_Spot.symptoms: Small brown pinpoint spots that enlarge to circular spots with light brown centers and dark rings. Severe defoliation can occur.
Tomato___Target_Spot.organic: Spray copper fungicides or Bacillus subtilis formulations. Mulch beds and trim lower leaves.
Tomato___Target_Spot.chemical: Apply Chlorothalonil, Azoxystrobin, or Boscalid fungicides.
Tomato___Target_Spot.prevention: Provide proper plant support (staking/trellising) to keep foliage off the damp ground, and rotate with non-solanaceous crops.

Tomato___Tomato_Yellow_Leaf_Curl_Virus.symptoms: Severe stunting of plants. Leaves curl upward and inward, turn bright yellow along the margins, and become small and cup-shaped.
Tomato___Tomato_Yellow_Leaf_Curl_Virus.organic: Control the whitefly carrier insect by using yellow sticky cards and spraying organic Neem oil or potassium soaps.
Tomato___Tomato_Yellow_Leaf_Curl_Virus.chemical: No chemical cure for the virus. Spray Imidacloprid or Acetamiprid insecticides to manage the whitefly vector population.
Tomato___Tomato_Yellow_Leaf_Curl_Virus.prevention: Grow plants under insect-proof nylon nets during seedling stage, immediately rogue (pull up and burn) infected plants, and control weeds.

Tomato___Tomato_mosaic_virus.symptoms: Mottled light and dark green mosaic patterns on leaves. Leaves may become narrow and string-like (fern-leaf symptom).
Tomato___Tomato_mosaic_virus.organic: Soak seeds in a 10% solution of trisodium phosphate (TSP) before sowing. Wash hands with soap and milk before handling plants.
Tomato___Tomato_mosaic_virus.chemical: No chemical treatment is effective against viruses. Remove and burn all infected plants immediately.
Tomato___Tomato_mosaic_virus.prevention: Disinfect garden tools with bleach, avoid smoking near tomato plants (tobacco can carry the virus), and plant resistant seed varieties.

healthy.symptoms: The leaf appears green, vibrant, and free from spots, discoloration, lesions, or insect webbing.
healthy.organic: Maintain healthy watering practices (early morning root watering). Apply organic compost or liquid seaweed fertilizer monthly.
healthy.chemical: No chemical application required. Avoid prophylactic fungicide sprays to preserve beneficial soil microbes.
healthy.prevention: Continue standard preventive care: clean weeding, crop rotation, and regular monitoring for early signs of disease.
